description_long: This is a large, single-edged machete-style knife with a pronounced forward curve and a broad blade. The blade features a series of circular cutouts near the spine, likely for weight reduction or aesthetic purposes. The edge is smooth and un-serrated, with a sharp, pointed tip designed for chopping or slashing. The handle is robust and ergonomically contoured, appearing to be made from a different material than the blade, possibly a synthetic or polymer for improved grip. The overall finish is matte black, giving the object a tactical and modern appearance. There is no visible guard between the blade and handle, and the design is utilitarian with minimal ornamentation.
description: A large, curved machete-style knife with a matte black blade featuring circular cutouts and a contoured handle. The design is modern, robust, and utilitarian.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full curve of the blade, circular cutouts, and the ergonomic handle.
description_view_1: Edge-on view highlighting the thinness of the blade and the rectangular profile of the handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring the blade's curve and handle details, with cutouts visible.
description_view_3: Edge-on view from the opposite side, showing the blade's thickness and handle's width.
attribute_blade_type: curved, single-edged, machete-style blade with circular cutouts
attribute_handle_design: contoured, polymer, ergonomic grip, matte finish
